perturbation.original_text: "SpinCo IP" means (a) the SpinCo Patents, (b) the SpinCo Copyrights, (c) the SpinCo Domain Names, (d) the SpinCo Trade Secrets, (e) the SpinCo Trademarks and (f) the SpinCo IDs.
perturbation.changed_text: "SpinCo IP" means (a) the SpinCo Patents, (b) the SpinCo Copyrights, (c) the SpinCo Domain Names, (d) the SpinCo Trade Secrets, (e) the SpinCo Trademarks and (f) the SpinCo Disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'SpinCo IP' to include 'SpinCo IDs'. Changing 'SpinCo IDs' to 'SpinCo Disclosures' creates an in-text contradiction, as the document uses 'SpinCo IDs' in other sections, but now the definition includes 'SpinCo Disclosures' without changing the rest of the agreement. This creates confusion regarding whether 'SpinCo IDs' and 'SpinCo Disclosures' are the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "SpinCo IDs" means the invention disclosures listed or described on Schedule B.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'SpinCo IDs' to 'SpinCo Disclosures' in the definition of 'SpinCo IP' directly contradicts the later use of 'SpinCo IDs' which is specifically defined as 'the invention disclosures listed or described on Schedule B'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the definition of SpinCo IP no longer includes SpinCo IDs, but another part of the agreement refers to 'SpinCo IDs'. According to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, the agreement uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, which leads to conflicting obligations. Changing the meaning of a term within the contract after that term has already been defined elsewhere causes a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event a Party divests a business by (a) spinning off a member of its Group by its sale or other disposition to a third party, (b) reducing ownership or control in a member of its Group so that it no longer qualifiers as a member of its Group under this Agreement or (c) selling or otherwise transferring a line of business to a third party (each such divested entity/line of business, a "Divested Entity"), the Divested Entity shall retain those licenses granted to it under this Agreement provided that the license shall be limited to the business of the Divested Entity as of the date of divestment and the natural development thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event a Party divests a business by (a) spinning off a member of its Group by its sale or other disposition to a third party, (b) reducing ownership or control in a member of its Group so that it no longer qualifiers as a member of its Group under this Agreement or (c) selling or otherwise transferring a line of business to a third party (each such divested entity/line of business, a "Spin-Off Entity"), the Divested Entity shall retain those licenses granted to it under this Agreement provided that the license shall be limited to the business of the Divested Entity as of the date of divestment and the natural development thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "Divested Entity" to "Spin-Off Entity" in Section 8.02, while still using "Divested Entity" in the subsequent sentences, creates a contradiction. This raises uncertainty regarding which definition applies to the continued use of "Divested Entity," especially concerning the rights and limitations of the entity retaining the licenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event a Party divests a business by (a) spinning off a member of its Group by its sale or other disposition to a third party, (b) reducing ownership or control in a member of its Group so that it no longer qualifiers as a member of its Group under this Agreement or (c) selling or otherwise transferring a line of business to a third party (each such divested entity/line of business, a "Spin-Off Entity"), the Divested Entity shall retain those licenses granted to it under this Agreement provided that the license shall be limited to the business of the Divested Entity as of the date of divestment and the natural development thereof. The retention of any license grants are subject to the Divested Entity's and, in the event it is acquired by a third party, such third party's execution and delivery to the non-transferring Party, within 90 days of the effective date of such divestment, of a duly authorized, written undertaking, agreeing to be bound by the applicable terms of this Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, in no event will the licenses retained by a Divested Entity extend to products, product lines, services, apparatus, devices, systems, components, hardware, software, processes, solutions, any combination of the foregoing, or other offerings of a third party acquirer existing on or before the date of the divestment, except to the extent that they were licensed under the terms of this Agreement prior to such divestment.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Divested Entity" to "Spin-Off Entity" in the definition creates a contradiction. The subsequent use of "Divested Entity" in the same sentence and throughout the section (as seen in the contradicted text) becomes ambiguous because the defining term has been altered for only part of the section. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the prompt, leads to uncertainty regarding which entity the agreement is referring to, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Trade Secrets and other confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement (including the Honeywell Content) shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement and may be used by the receiving Party for the express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: All Trade Secrets and other confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement (including the Honeywell Materials) shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement and may be used by the receiving Party for the express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to "Honeywell Content" within the clause describing confidential information. By changing "Honeywell Content" to "Honeywell Materials", while "Honeywell Content" is defined and used elsewhere in the agreement (e.g., Section 3.03), we introduce a contradiction. This creates ambiguity about what specific materials are subject to the confidentiality clause, because the content being reffered in this section 6.01 is not same as in section 3.03. Are 'Honeywell Materials' intended to be the same as, different from, or a subset of 'Honeywell Content'? This affects the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Honeywell Content. (a) Notwithstanding Section 3.01, Honeywell hereby grants, and agrees to cause the members of the Honeywell Group to hereby grant, to SpinCo and the members of the SpinCo Group, for a period of ten (10) years after the Distribution Date (unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 3.03(c)), a non-exclusive, royalty-free, fully-paid, non-sublicenseable, non-transferable, worldwide license to use and reproduce the Honeywell Content solely for the SpinCo Group's internal business purposes. For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge and agree that SpinCo may distribute the Honeywell Content internally through SpinCo's intranet in the same form and manner that it was distributed on the Honeywell intranet immediately prior to the Distribution Date; provided, that the Honeywell Content may not be used for any purpose other than the SpinCo Group's internal business purposes and may not be shared with any third party without the prior written consent of Honeywell. (b) SpinCo shall, and shall cause each member of the SpinCo Group to, remove any Honeywell Trademarks or reference to the Honeywell Business appearing on any Honeywell Content as soon as reasonably practicable following the Distribution Date, but in no event later than one hundred and eighty (180) days after the Distribution Date. (c) Without limiting ARTICLE VIII, the license granted to the SpinCo Group in Section 3.03(a) shall automatically terminate in the event (i) that any member of the SpinCo Group assigns, transfers, licenses or otherwise conveys any rights in or to the Honeywell Content to any third party or (ii) of (x) the sale of all or substantially all of the ownership interests in, or the assets of, any member of the SpinCo Group in a single transaction or a series of related transactions to one or more third parties, (y) any direct or indirect acquisition, consolidation or merger of any member of the SpinCo Group by, with or into any third party or (z) any spin-off, public offering or other corporate reorganization or single transaction or series of related transactions in which direct or indirect control of any member of the SpinCo Group is transferred to one or more third parties, including by transferring an excess of fifty percent (50%) of such member of the SpinCo Group's voting power, shares or equity, through a merger, consolidation, tender offer or similar transaction to one or more third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Honeywell Content,' which is specifically defined in Section 3.03 and granted a license for use. The changed text replaces 'Honeywell Content' with 'Honeywell Materials,' which is undefined. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts Section 3.03 because it's unclear whether 'Honeywell Materials' is the same as, different from, or a subset of the defined 'Honeywell Content.' This misalignment of terminology creates a direct contradiction because the confidentiality obligations now apply to an undefined term, while a specific term ('Honeywell Content') is used elsewhere in the contract to define rights and obligations, including licensing (section 3.03).
